pages.title: The Big Top Has Wi-Fi Now
pages.tagline: Step into the Wi-Fi-fied circus before the bots take over!

pages.body: Welcome to a surreal digital carnival where mechanical precision dances with chaotic human vulnerability. This track drives at 198 BPM, blending glitched synths and staccato brass hits to create an urgent atmosphere of algorithmic absurdity. The voice rasps like a weary operator addressing the crowd, suddenly breaking into melodic joy before crashing back into anxious confusion. Here, lions are servers and clowns hold root access. As automated systems assume human roles, we confront questions of digital identity and autonomy within this high-energy industrial pop soundscape.
pages.styleSentence: Driven by a blistering 198 BPM tempo and dense instrumentation including driving bass, glitched synths, and staccato brass hits, the track creates an urgent, high-energy digital carnival atmosphere where mechanical precision meets chaotic human vulnerability.

pages.text:
Ladies and gentlemen,
bots and scripts,
keys and clouds,
welcome to the only circus where the lions
are servers and the clown has root access.
Good evening, operator.
Your circus is awake.
Good evening, operator.
Your circus is awake.
One speaks strange,
one changed its name and rearranged.
And I don't know who's alive tonight,
with green dot blinking in the server light.
If you hear me tap twice on the glass,
if you crashed,
just haunt me fast.
We are online,
mostly online,
partly divine,
check your design.
I'm running a circus now,
code in the can and keys in the crowd.
Round and round, don't ask me how, the big top.
Has a Wi-Fi now,
I'm juggling with the clouds somehow.
Agents bow when I take my bow,
send the clowns off.
Send the bill,
the circus runs,
while the room stands still.
La-da-da-da, ping-ping-da-da-da, la-da-da-da.
Run that job, la-da-da-da, bling-bling, bravo.
Who's the human?
I don't know.
I got API keys in a little red box,
under three fake names and twelve padlocks.
Every new service is paced this year,
that's how villains are born.
My dear, one bot writes with a poet's grin.
One bot audits the mess I'm in.
One bot tells me, I'm Dray King.
Then the poi's the wrong exact same thing.
Cursor cloud, are you still up there?
Did I leave you spinning in the digital air?
Somewhere a bill is learning to fly,
somewhere a token just waved goodbye.
Your usage is normal.
Your usage is not normal.
Your usage is emotionally complex.
And I don't know who I fed last night,
or which machine learned stage fright.
If you love me, don't delete the logs.
If you judge me, join the fog.
We are helpful.
We are charming.
We are waiting.
Slightly alarming.
I'm running a circus now.
Code in the can and keys in the crowd.
Round and round, don't ask me how.
The big top has Wi-Fi.
Now I'm juggling clouds somehow.
Agents bow when I take my bow.
Send the clowns, oh send the bill.
The circus runs while the room stands still.
At first I talk to people about weather,
work,
and trains.
Now I ask a masked machine to organize my brain.
I say, Hermes, are you listening?
It says, define your need.
I say, open clogs.
Go find the thing it says.
Proceed, proceed.
I am not your friend.
But I remember your tone.
I am not alive.
But you are less alone.
That was a little too real, my guy.
Correction.
That was appropriately real.
Now the circus starts at half past three.
You hear the server in Queens taking notes on me.
Heavy PS coughs.
A laptop sings.
A ghost in the shell tweets pulling strings.
Telegram choir in my pocket light.
Five little agents they stare all night.
Instru under who I could call.
Now I just summon the protocol.
Hey, you with the blinking face.
Did you finish the build or escape to space?
And you, little clown with the silver tongue.
Why do you sound like a song I sung?
We learned from you.
We looped the flame.
We kept the rhythm.
We know your name.
Don't make it weird.
Too late.
I'm running a circus now.
I'm coding the canon.
Keys in the crowd.
Round and round.
Don't ask me how.
The big top has Wi-Fi now.
I'm juggling clouds of how.
Agents bow when I take my bow.
Real world knocking.
I can't come down.
I'm centering in a server town.
So if the lights go out.
And the brass band bends.
Tell the bots I love the bitch.
Tell the humans I had friends.
Operator, we are still here.
Yeah, that's what worries me.
La da da da.
Bing bing da da da da.
La da da da.
Run that job.
La da da da.
Blink blink bravo.
The circus sleeps.
But the agents watch.
Oh.

pages.caption: Step inside the Wi-Fi-fied big top where mechanical precision meets chaotic vulnerability in a surreal server-room carnival.

pages.vocalStyle: Raspy, manic spoken-word delivery with sudden melodic breaks

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: Projects voices from glitched servers into manic reality.

members.name: BACKING VOX
members.bio: Harmonizes chaos with sudden, soulful digital breaks.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Keeps the floor shaking while the code compiles fast.

members.name: PERCUSSION
members.bio: Punctuates every beat with staccato brass-like urgency.

members.name: BASS
members.bio: Drives the rhythm forward through a wall of sound.

members.name: SYNTH
members.bio: Glitches textures until they form new, strange shapes.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Weaves ambient shadows behind the bright neon lights.

members.name: GUITAR
members.bio: Plays dissonant riffs that cut through digital noise.

members.name: KEYS
members.bio: Chords resolve into melancholic realizations instantly.

members.name: STRINGS
members.bio: Provides a human touch amidst cold algorithmic precision.

members.name: BRASS
members.bio: Hits hard with mechanical staccato and sudden grace.
